pokemon:boldore.name: Boldore
pokemon:boldore.summary: When it is healthy, its core sticks out. Always facing the same way, it swiftly moves front to back and left to right.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: When it is healthy, its core sticks out. Always facing the same way, it swiftly moves front to back and left to right.
When it overflows with power, the orange crystal on its body glows. It looks for underground water in caves.
When it is healthy, its core sticks out. Always facing the same way, it swiftly moves front to back and left to right.
It explores caves in search of underground water. It’s not comfortable around water, so this Pokémon takes great care in lapping it up.
Because its energy was too great to be contained, the energy leaked and formed orange crystals.
It relies on sound in order to monitor what’s in its vicinity. When angered, it will attack without ever changing the direction it’s facing.
Its orange crystal is a mass of energy. Just one crystal fragment would provide enough fuel for a hundred dump trucks.
If you see its orange crystals start to glow, be wary. It’s about to fire off bursts of energy.
Its orange crystals are lumps of powerful energy. They’re valuable, so Boldore is sometimes targeted for them.
The energy overflowing from its body has turned into orange crystals that are hard enough to smash diamonds.
Because its energy was too great to be contained, the energy leaked and formed orange crystals.
When it is healthy, its core sticks out. Always facing the same way, it swiftly moves front to back and left to right.
Because its energy was too great to be contained, the energy leaked and formed orange crystals.
When it is healthy, its core sticks out. Always facing the same way, it swiftly moves front to back and left to right.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: When it is healthy, its core sticks out. Always facing the same way, it swiftly moves front to back and left to right.

pokemon:roggenrola.name: Roggenrola
pokemon:roggenrola.summary: They were discovered a hundred years ago in an earthquake fissure. Inside each one is an energy core.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: They were discovered a hundred years ago in an earthquake fissure. Inside each one is an energy core.
Its ear is hexagonal in shape. Compressed underground, its body is as hard as steel.
The hexagonal-shaped hole is its ear. It has a tendency to walk in the direction the sound is coming from.
The hexagonal cavity is its ear. It walks in the direction of sounds it hears, but if the sounds cease, it panics and topples over.
Its ear is hexagonal in shape. Compressed underground, its body is as hard as steel.
When it detects a noise, it starts to move. The energy core inside it makes this Pokémon slightly warm to the touch.
Born deep within the ground, it compares itself with similar species, such as Geodude and Carbink, to settle which has the hardest body.
It’s as hard as steel, but apparently a long soak in water will cause it to soften a bit.
It was found in a fissure in a layer of exposed rock. The material that makes up its body is dirt from several hundred years ago.
The hexagonal hole is its ear. Deep in that ear is an energy core, so if you stick your hand in there, Roggenrola will be very angry!
They were discovered a hundred years ago in an earthquake fissure. Inside each one is an energy core.
The hexagonal-shaped hole is its ear. It has a tendency to walk in the direction the sound is coming from.
Its ear is hexagonal in shape. Compressed underground, its body is as hard as steel.
They were discovered a hundred years ago in an earthquake fissure. Inside each one is an energy core.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: They were discovered a hundred years ago in an earthquake fissure. Inside each one is an energy core.

pokemon:gigalith.name: Gigalith
pokemon:gigalith.summary: Compressing the energy from its internal core lets it fire off an attack capable of blowing away a mountain.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Compressing the energy from its internal core lets it fire off an attack capable of blowing away a mountain.
Compressing the energy from its internal core lets it fire off an attack capable of blowing away a mountain.
The solar rays it absorbs are processed in its energy core and fired as a ball of light.
Known for its hefty horsepower, this Pokémon is a popular partner for construction workers.
The solar rays it absorbs are processed in its energy core and fired as a ball of light.
Although its energy blasts can blow away a dump truck, they have a limitation— they can only be fired when the sun is out.
The blasts of energy it makes from sunbeams have terrifying power. However, it’s not able to fire its blasts at night or on rainy days.
This hardy Pokémon can often be found on construction sites and in mines, working alongside people and Copperajah.
It absorbs rays of sunlight and shoots out energy. It’s usually lurking deep beneath the surface.
When it fires off energy with all its might, the power creates countless fissures on its body.
The solar energy absorbed by its body’s orange crystals is magnified internally and fired from its mouth.
The solar rays it absorbs are processed in its energy core and fired as a ball of light.
The solar rays it absorbs are processed in its energy core and fired as a ball of light.
Compressing the energy from its internal core lets it fire off an attack capable of blowing away a mountain.

loreSummary: Compressing the energy from its internal core lets it fire off an attack capable of blowing away a mountain.
